source.public_copy_note: This public context JSON was generated from the live Google Sheet. Local machine paths, local device ports, and Foundry media_reference payloads are omitted/redacted in the public copy. The published LeRobot parquet/video files remain in the EP_017 and EP_018 folders.

rows.notes: DC_G collected EP_017 cleanly, then switched scenario and collected EP_018 after a short setup interval; EP_018 had a localized pick-object drop failure.

rows.example_source: Generic leader/follower teleoperation using a leader arm and follower robot arm
rows.notes: Leader/follower is a teleoperation method. The follower robot may provide wrist/body cameras while external top/front views are also captured. Modality details are tracked in run-level bundles.

LeRobot SO-101 is a low-cost single-arm tabletop manipulator used with the LeRobot ecosystem for real-world manipulation data collection. In this workbook it represents Abby's actual SO-101 collection context for pick/place scenarios. It is modeled as a stationary manipulator with a simple two-finger gripper; failures should be interpreted around grasp/contact/release quality rather than mobile navigation.

Home Wi-Fi environment used for representative SO-101 collection scenarios. Internet connection was stable and fast enough that upload delay/retry was not a meaningful collection bottleneck; network should not be treated as the source of failure for these episodes.

rows.task_goal: Move the target object into the target bin and leave it stably placed without drop, collision, or wrong-zone placement.
rows.task_definition_notes: Task is a reusable semantic template independent of collection method. This table defines the overall task goal; subtask-level preconditions/success postconditions and execution order are managed in 07_SubtaskOrder.

rows.notes: Real SO-101 LF baseline pick/place episode with one centered small cube and no distractors.

rows.success_postcondition: End-effector approaches the target object without collision and keeps the target visible.
rows.notes: Defines the ordered reusable subtasks inside each task.

rows.success_postcondition: End-effector aligns to a plausible grasp pose before contact.
End-effector reaches the target object without unsafe contact or loss of tracking.
Object is grasped and lifted without slip, drop, wrong-object pickup, or tracking loss.
Object is carried toward the target bin without collision, drop, or unstable motion.
Object is released inside the target bin and remains stable.
Final state confirms the object is in the correct bin with no drop or collision.

EP_017 completed successfully with all seven subtask segments usable for training. Clean pass export package; no issue segment is attached.

DC_G collected EP_017 cleanly and EP_018 as partial salvage. 13/14 segments are usable_for_training in 10A: EP_017 contributes 7 clean segments; EP_018 contributes 3 pre-failure + 3 post-recovery usable segments, with SEG_018_04 excluded after two collector-caused pick-object drops. Yield is healthy, but the repeated pick drop indicates targeted robot-operation practice is needed.

rows.recommended_action_detail: Give DC_G targeted robot-operation practice for SUB_PICK_PLACE_04: repeat grasp stabilization drills on the irregular object until two consecutive practice episodes have zero drop_events_in_pick_subtask.
rows.notes: Action is linked only to DEF_018_04 / QC_018_SENSOR_AUTO_QC. EP_017 was clean; EP_018 failed locally because the collector dropped the object twice while operating the robot. This is a coaching/practice recommendation, not a robot-platform or network remediation.
